summary: The patient presents significant conjugated hyperbilirubinemia without changes in general hepatic enzymes suggesting a primary transport disorder like Rotor Syndrome as opposed to more common conditions such as Gilbert’s disease where unconjugated levels are elevated alongwith total serum bilirubin increase. Given that there were no additional findings pointing towards obstruction or hemolysis, this further cements diagnosis of Rotor Syndrome given its specific presentation matching provided lab results closely. Thus, based purely off presented data, diagnosing Patient with Rotor Syndrome seems most appropriate among available choices considering both biochemical profile & exclusion criteria met for
